title: helictotrichon friesiorum
intro: Helictotrichon friesiorum, also known as Fries' false brome or Fries' hairgrass, is a type of ornamental grass native to Europe, commonly used in landscaping due to its attractive blue-gray foliage and delicate white flowers. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including the ability to lower blood pressure and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Consuming Helictotrichon friesiorum has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Helictotrichon friesiorum are typically made from the dried leaves and flowers, and are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
